title: Fast Fiction Factory Designer Candidate Dashboard

content_candidates.premise_audience_promise: A clockmaker hears a removed bell ring and finds a clue to stolen lives.
content_candidates.visual_or_thumbnail_direction: Empty bell tower, brass moth detail, stark noon shadow.
content_candidates.subtitle_or_narration_promise: Short mystery lines that keep the impossible image clear.
content_candidates.recommended_next_review_decision: Approve or revise the bell paradox as the first visible hook.

content_candidates.title_or_hook: Mira and the brass moth clue trail
content_candidates.premise_audience_promise: A personal mystery follows Toma's note into the council ledger.
content_candidates.visual_or_thumbnail_direction: Workshop closeups, note fragment, moth prop, ledger page, station exterior.
content_candidates.subtitle_or_narration_promise: Narration can promise family stakes without revealing Toma's fate.
content_candidates.recommended_next_review_decision: Choose the first-use behavior for the moth or keep this candidate held.

content_candidates.title_or_hook: The city that sells abandoned minutes
content_candidates.premise_audience_promise: A lyrical dark-fantasy explainer frames the moral cost of stolen time.
content_candidates.visual_or_thumbnail_direction: Ledger of names beside a clock missing one hour.
content_candidates.subtitle_or_narration_promise: Can explain the premise while marking ending and motive as provisional.
content_candidates.recommended_next_review_decision: Review whether the premise can be explained before the council route is chosen.

channel_strategy_proposals.target_audience: viewers who like atmospheric speculative hooks and compact emotional turns
channel_strategy_proposals.series_angle: Each short opens with one impossible civic object and one private cost.
channel_strategy_proposals.cadence_hypothesis: 2 to 3 short concepts per week after the review workflow stabilizes.
channel_strategy_proposals.material_fit: The current story already has a repeatable hook, object clue, and unresolved ending pressure.
channel_strategy_proposals.review_debt: Decide whether the channel leans poetic, mystery-forward, or production-design-forward.

channel_strategy_proposals.target_audience: viewers who follow clues, institutions, and hidden-world rules across episodes
channel_strategy_proposals.series_angle: A case-file sequence where each recovered minute exposes a different erased life.
channel_strategy_proposals.cadence_hypothesis: weekly short plus occasional 3-minute recap after canon routes are chosen
channel_strategy_proposals.material_fit: The source material has unresolved motives and repeatable evidence objects.
channel_strategy_proposals.review_debt: Needs a spoiler policy before longer recap formats.

channel_strategy_proposals.target_audience: writers and designers reviewing story-to-video packaging choices
channel_strategy_proposals.series_angle: Show how a raw fiction memo becomes reviewable video candidates without publishing.
channel_strategy_proposals.cadence_hypothesis: one dashboard review episode per completed local story slice
channel_strategy_proposals.material_fit: The project already exposes source, claim, profile, timeline, and risk surfaces.
channel_strategy_proposals.review_debt: Needs a repeatable review card once a second story exists.

draft_spine.sample_opening_status: style sample only; not final prose

beats.label: Hook
beats.beat: At noon, the observatory rings although the bell is gone.
beats.purpose: Shows the impossible image before any explanation.

beats.label: Inciting clue
beats.beat: Mira finds Toma's note and the brass moth in the workshop drawer.
beats.purpose: Connects family stakes to the stolen-minutes premise.

beats.label: Investigation
beats.beat: The ledger points toward lives removed from public memory.
beats.purpose: Lets the premise become inspectable through an object, not exposition.

beats.label: Motive pressure
beats.beat: The council route stays open: villainous, desperate, divided, misled, or false-record.
beats.purpose: Prevents the dashboard from deciding story truth.

beats.label: Ending candidate
beats.beat: Mira may return one lost hour to each erased person.
beats.purpose: Surfaces the emotional endpoint while keeping final canon held.

review_risks.finding: Toma fate, brass moth truth, and Council motive remain unresolved.
review_risks.next_move: Keep content candidates held until the author chooses provisional routes.

review_risks.finding: Thumbnail and visual directions are text-only placeholders with unknown rights.
review_risks.next_move: Do not source release assets or imply rights clearance in this slice.

review_risks.finding: No render, upload, provider call, or video generation exists.
review_risks.next_move: Treat dashboard output as local planning readback only.

review_risks.finding: Draft spine is reviewable but not final prose.
review_risks.next_move: Use it to decide structure before writing a full draft.

validation_notes: Designer Dashboard is a local planning and readback surface only.
Content cards, channel proposals, and draft beats remain review-held.
Unknown or human-owned story truth remains visible instead of being invented.
No provider, credential, public upload, AI video generation, production render, or final canon decision is added.

absence_and_unknowns_visible.detail: unknowns=true; label=not available / needs human review
